items.title: Autocomplete at scale
items.scenario: You type "The capital of France is" into a small phone keyboard.
items.explanation: The keyboard suggests "Paris" from local patterns. An LLM does analogous next-token prediction, but learned from vastly more text and context—so it can continue entire paragraphs, not just one word.

items.title: Why pasted docs matter
items.scenario: You ask Claude to summarize a 20-page PDF you attach in chat.
items.explanation: Claude cannot recall the PDF from pretraining; it must read the tokens you provide in context. If the doc exceeds the window, you must chunk or use a long-context workflow.

items.title: Prompt sensitivity
items.scenario: Same question asked as "List risks" vs "List risks as a table with severity ratings."
items.explanation: Both hit the same model weights, but different prompts activate different continuation patterns—format and depth change without any code change.
